info.title: speechapi
info.description: Speech APIs enable you to recognize speech and convert it to text using advanced machine learning, and also to convert text to speech.

post.summary: Recognize audio input as text using Advanced AI
post.description: Uses advanced AI to convert input audio to text. Supports WAV, MP3, M4A, FLAC, OGG, and WMA formats. Consumes 1 API call per second of audio in Fast mode, 5 API calls per second in Normal mode, and 10 API calls per second in Advanced mode.

parameters.name: languageCode
parameters.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter language code (e.g. eng, spa, fra). Empty for auto-detect.

parameters.name: recognitionMode
parameters.description: Recognition mode: Fast, Normal (default), or Advanced. Advanced is only available on Private Cloud and Managed Instance deployments.

post.summary: Recognize audio input as text using Advanced AI with high-accuracy word-level timestamps
post.description:
Higher-accuracy variant of /speech/recognize/file. Uses advanced
alignment for token timestamps.
Supports WAV, MP3, M4A, FLAC, OGG, and WMA formats.

parameters.name: languageCode
parameters.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter language code (e.g. eng, spa, fra). Empty for auto-detect.

parameters.name: recognitionMode
parameters.description: Recognition mode: Fast, Normal (default), or Advanced. Advanced is only available on Private Cloud and Managed Instance deployments.

post.summary: Generate audio from text using Advanced AI
post.description: Converts text to speech using advanced AI. Supports English, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, and Chinese. Specify language with LanguageCode (ISO 639-3, default: eng) and gender with Gender (Male or Female, default: Female). Output format is controlled by the Format field (mp3 or wav, default: mp3). Consumes 1 API call per second of generated audio.

post.summary: Recognize audio input as text using Advanced AI as a Batch Job
post.description: Creates an async batch job for transcribing a long audio file.  Use the GetAsyncJobStatus API to check on the status of the job and retrieve the result when complete.  Uses advanced AI to convert input audio to text.  Supports WAV, MP3, M4A, FLAC, OGG, and WMA formats.  Consumes 1 API call per second of audio in Fast mode, 5 API calls per second in Normal mode, and 10 API calls per second in Advanced mode.  Requires Managed Instance or Private Cloud deployment.

parameters.name: languageCode
parameters.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter language code (e.g. eng, spa, fra). Empty for auto-detect.

parameters.name: recognitionMode
parameters.description: Recognition mode: Fast, Normal (default), or Advanced. Advanced is only available on Private Cloud and Managed Instance deployments.

post.summary: Recognize audio input as text using Advanced AI with high-accuracy word-level timestamps as a Batch Job
post.description: Creates an async batch job for transcribing a long audio file with high-accuracy word-level timestamps.  Use the GetAsyncJobStatus API to check on the status of the job and retrieve the result when complete.  Higher-accuracy variant of /speech/batch-job/recognize/file. Uses advanced alignment for token timestamps.  Supports WAV, MP3, M4A, FLAC, OGG, and WMA formats.  Consumes 1 API call per second of audio in Fast mode, 5 API calls per second in Normal mode, and 10 API calls per second in Advanced mode.  Requires Managed Instance or Private Cloud deployment.

parameters.name: languageCode
parameters.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter language code (e.g. eng, spa, fra). Empty for auto-detect.

parameters.name: recognitionMode
parameters.description: Recognition mode: Fast, Normal (default), or Advanced. Advanced is only available on Private Cloud and Managed Instance deployments.

post.summary: Generate audio from text using Advanced AI as a Batch Job
post.description: Creates an async batch job for converting long-form text to speech.  Use the GetAsyncJobStatus API to check on the status of the job and retrieve the generated audio when complete.  Converts text to speech using advanced AI.  Supports English, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, and Chinese.  Specify language with LanguageCode (ISO 639-3, default: eng) and gender with Gender (Male or Female, default: Female).  Output format is controlled by the Format field (mp3 or wav, default: mp3).  Consumes 1 API call per second of generated audio.  Requires Managed Instance or Private Cloud deployment.

get.summary: Get the status and result of a Speech Batch Job
get.description: Returns the result of the Async Job - possible states can be STARTED or COMPLETED.  When COMPLETED, the corresponding result field (Recognize transcription, or generated Text-to-Speech audio) is populated on the response.  This API is only available for Cloudmersive Managed Instance and Private Cloud deployments.

parameters.name: AsyncJobID
parameters.description: Job ID for the batch job to get the status of

SpeechBatchJobResult.description: Result of submitting a Speech batch job

AsyncJobID.description: When creating a job, an Async Job ID is returned.  Use the GetAsyncJobStatus API to check on the status of this job using the AsyncJobID and get the result when it finishes

SpeechBatchJobStatusResult.description: Result of performing a Speech batch job operation

Successful.description: True if the operation to check the status of the job was successful, false otherwise

AsyncJobStatus.description: Returns the job status of the Async Job, if applicable.  Possible states are STARTED and COMPLETED

SpeakAudioResultFileContent.description: Generated audio file produced by a completed Text-to-Speech batch job, in the requested format (mp3 or wav)

SpeakAudioContentType.description: MIME type of SpeakAudioResultFileContent (audio/mpeg or audio/wav)

TextResult.example: Hello world, this is a test.

Timestamps.description: Token-level timestamps with character offsets into TextResult

SpeechRecognitionResultAdvanced.description:
Result of recognizing speech with the higher-accuracy file/advanced endpoint.
Word-oriented (one entry per word, not per BPE sub-token) and produced
from DTW-aligned timestamps.

TextResult.example: Hello world, this is a test.

Words.description:
Word-level timestamps with character offsets into TextResult.
Timestamps are derived via Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) alignment for
substantially better accuracy than the default token-timestamp path.

Text.description: Text to be converted to speech
Text.example: Hello, this is a test of the text to speech system.

Format.description: File format for output audio file: wav or mp3, default is mp3

LanguageCode.description: ISO 639-3 three-letter language code (e.g. eng, spa, fra, hin, ita, jpn, por, zho). Default is eng (English).

Gender.description: Voice gender: Male or Female. Default is Female. Note: Male is not available for French and Chinese.

TokenTimestamp.description: A single token with its time range and position in the output text

StartTimeMs.description: Start time of this token in the audio, in milliseconds

EndTimeMs.description: End time of this token in the audio, in milliseconds

WordTimestamp.description: A single word with its DTW-aligned time range and position in the output text.

StartTimeMs.description: Start time of this word in the audio, in milliseconds

EndTimeMs.description: End time of this word in the audio, in milliseconds
